citations.narrative:
Enclosures are not being cleaned properly. The walls of approximately 6 sheltered primary enclosures housing
approximately 14 adult dogs are soiled with brown material. Additionally, several of the doors leading to the outside runs
also are soiled with a brown material. Inadequate and infrequent cleaning and sanitization allows for the growth of
disease-causing agents that may increase the risk of health hazards for the animals. Appropriate cleaning and sanitization
of the surfaces must include removal of the buildup of material found and to prevent accumulation of this material. All
surfaces of housing facilities must be cleaned and sanitized when necessary to satisfy generally accepted husbandry
standards and practices. Correct by ensuring hard surfaces with which the dogs come into contact with are spot cleaned
daily and sanitized in accordance with 3.11 (b) of this subpart to prevent accumulation of excreta and reduce disease
hazards. Correct by 13-APRIL-2026.

The facility is not following the written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC):
-The PVC states to administer Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines yearly for adult dogs. For adult female French Bulldog
(#47146), the animal last received the Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines on 1/3/2025.
-The PVC states that 10% of litters will be fecal tested each year. Fecal sampling for puppies is not being performed.
Failure to administer required vaccines and conduct sampling in accordance with a veterinary approved schedule may
result in animals to not be adequately protected against disease and/or can cause pain and distress. Correct by ensuring
all animals are vaccinated and sampled in accordance with the schedule approved by the Attending Veterinarian and
contact the Attending Veterinarian to review records and create a plan to determine when to administer future
vaccinations for the applicable animals. Correct by 13-APRIL-2026.

The facility does not maintain complete medical records for all dogs. The facility states that three French Bulldog puppies
(#1101, #1102, #1103) received a Parvovirus vaccine at 8 weeks of age but they could not provide documentation.
Failure to maintain adequate medical records may interfere with the ability of APHIS officials to determine if the animals
are receiving adequate veterinary care. Complete medical records must be maintained and include the names of all
vaccines and treatments administered and the dates of administration. Correct by documenting all treatments given and
ensure that medical records are complete and contain the required information. Correct by 13-APRIL-2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
n

narrative:
3.1(c)(3) 
   
Housing facilities, general. 
Enclosures are not being cleaned properly. The walls of approximately 6 sheltered primary enclosures housing 
approximately 14 adult dogs are soiled with brown material. Additionally, several of the doors leading to the outside runs 
also are soiled with a brown material. Inadequate and infrequent cleaning and sanitization allows for the growth of 
disease-causing agents that may increase the risk of health hazards for the animals. Appropriate cleaning and sanitization 
of the surfaces must include removal of the buildup of material found and to prevent accumulation of this material. All 
surfaces of housing facilities must be cleaned and sanitized when necessary to satisfy generally accepted husbandry 
standards and practices. Correct by ensuring hard surfaces with which the dogs come into contact with are spot cleaned 
daily and sanitized in accordance with 3.11 (b) of this subpart to prevent accumulation of excreta and reduce disease 
hazards. Correct by 13-APRIL-2026. 
3.13(a)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The facility is not following the written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC): 
 
-The PVC states to administer Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines yearly for adult dogs. For adult female French Bulldog 
(#47146), the animal last received the Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines on 1/3/2025. 
 
-The PVC states that 10% of litters will be fecal tested each year. Fecal sampling for puppies is not being performed. 
 
Failure to administer required vaccines and conduct sampling in accordance with a veterinary approved schedule may 
result in animals to not be adequately protected against disease and/or can cause pain and distress. Correct by ensuring 
all animals are vaccinated and sampled in accordance with the schedule approved by the Attending Veterinarian and 
contact the Attending Veterinarian to review records and create a plan to determine when to administer future 
vaccinations for the applicable animals. Correct by 13-APRIL-2026. 
3.13(b)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs.

The facility does not maintain complete medical records for all dogs. The facility states that three French Bulldog puppies 
(#1101, #1102, #1103) received a Parvovirus vaccine at 8 weeks of age but they could not provide documentation. 
Failure to maintain adequate medical records may interfere with the ability of APHIS officials to determine if the animals 
are receiving adequate veterinary care. Complete medical records must be maintained and include the names of all 
vaccines and treatments administered and the dates of administration. Correct by documenting all treatments given and 
ensure that medical records are complete and contain the required information. Correct by 13-APRIL-2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
